evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Datenna is a fast-growing tech scale-up... Working closely with stakeholders, you'll ensure data quality and governance while optimizing performance.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD combines scale-up/fast-growing speed framing with explicit data quality, governance, and CI/CD rigour language in roughly equal measure.
evidence.domain_risk: Datenna is a fast-growing tech scale-up combining cutting-edge OSINT and AI technologies to provide governments with critical insights into China's techno-economic landscape.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The product serves government clients with geopolitical intelligence, a business-decision context without direct financial-regulatory compliance stakes for the analytics engineering role itself, so moderate rather than high.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Develop and maintain data transformation pipelines using dbt, SQL, and Python... Experience with orchestrations frameworks, such as Dagster or Airflow.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The JD references an existing named tool stack (dbt, Databricks, Dagster/Airflow) to maintain and optimize at a scale-up, indicating an established stack that is actively growing rather than a first build or a large mature function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design efficient data models using techniques like star schema and snowflake schema for analytics and reporting.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The JD lists specific tools (dbt, Databricks, Python, Dagster/Airflow) and modelling techniques a hiring manager would specify, mixed with more generic language like "collaborate with stakeholders."
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data requirements and implement solutions.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Stakeholders are referenced generically without naming a specific commercial, product, or finance function, so mixed applies.
evidence.autonomy_level: Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data requirements and implement solutions.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Responsibilities are framed as implementing and maintaining solutions defined by stakeholder requirements, an execution orientation, though the candidate is expected to bring their own technical judgment.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Your expertise in data warehouses and CI/CD practices will be crucial in building a robust analytics infrastructure that drives decision-making across the organization.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role builds and extends analytics infrastructure at a scale-up but references existing pipelines, tools, and practices to optimize rather than a first-hire/from-zero mandate.
evidence.ai_role: Experience with AI coding assistants such as Claude Code or Codex is a plus; We use AI tools extensively to move faster and raise quality... We expect every candidate to be comfortable using AI in their day-to-day work
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is explicitly expected to use AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex) to accelerate their own work, a clear ai_user signal.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement data quality checks and governance practices
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality checks and governance are framed as an owned responsibility (implement) rather than a tool listed in a skills section.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: ensure data quality and governance while optimizing performance; Implement data quality checks and governance practices
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data quality and governance are named concerns but are secondary to delivery (building pipelines, optimizing performance) with no explicit compliance/regulatory framing for the AE role itself, so this reads as moderate rather than high.
